visuals.title: Decomposition Techniques in Software Project Management
visuals.description: Animated visual showing WBS hierarchy, LOC estimation, Function Point analysis, COCOMO effort model, and benefits of decomposition for software project estimation.

diagrams.title: Work Breakdown Structure Flow
diagrams.description: Hierarchical decomposition of a project into phases, tasks, and subtasks

steps.label: Project
steps.description: Define the overall software project scope

steps.label: Phases
steps.description: Divide into major phases (Requirements, Design, Implementation, Testing)

steps.label: Tasks
steps.description: Break each phase into specific tasks

steps.label: Subtasks
steps.description: Further decompose until each piece is estimable

diagrams.title: Decomposition-Based Estimation Approaches
diagrams.description: Flow of size-based (LOC) and function-based (FP) estimation techniques

steps.label: Decompose Software Scope
steps.description: Break the problem into smaller functions or components

steps.label: LOC Estimation
steps.description: Estimate lines of code per function, then sum totals

steps.label: Function Point Estimation
steps.description: Count EI, EO, EQ, ILF, EIF and apply complexity weights

steps.label: Effort Estimation (COCOMO)
steps.description: Use E = a(KLOC)^b at Basic, Intermediate, or Detailed level

steps.label: Project Schedule & Cost
steps.description: Derive schedule and cost from effort estimates
